item.title:
RECESSION ODDS SURGE TO 36% 📈
Kalshi shows the probability of a U.S. recession this year has jumped nearly 15 points in weeks, now at 36%. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
RECESSION ODDS SURGE TO 36% 📈

Kalshi shows the probability of a U.S. recession this year has jumped nearly 15 points in weeks, now at 36%.
kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnb…

item.title:
POWELL EXPECTED TO WARN OF STAGFLATION RISKS
Ahead of the March Fed meeting, Jerome Powell is likely to acknowledge stagflation risks as oil prices surge, according to Bank of America.
The Summary of Economic Projections is expected to show higher headline and core inflation, with longer-run growth and rate projections ticking up, while Powell signals a cautious, wait-and-see approach.
item.description:
POWELL EXPECTED TO WARN OF STAGFLATION RISKS

Ahead of the March Fed meeting, Jerome Powell is likely to acknowledge stagflation risks as oil prices surge, according to Bank of America.

The Summary of Economic Projections is expected to show higher headline and core inflation, with longer-run growth and rate projections ticking up, while Powell signals a cautious, wait-and-see approach.

item.title:
GAS PRICES JUMP IN COLORADO AMID IRAN OIL CRUNCH
Gas prices in parts of Colorado, including the Denver area, are climbing toward about $3.89 per gallon, according to gas price expert Patrick De Haan — a move linked to higher oil prices from the ongoing Iran‑related crisis and disruptions to global supply
item.description:
GAS PRICES JUMP IN COLORADO AMID IRAN OIL CRUNCH

Gas prices in parts of Colorado, including the Denver area, are climbing toward about $3.89 per gallon, according to gas price expert Patrick De Haan — a move linked to higher oil prices from the ongoing Iran‑related crisis and disruptions to global supply

item.title: Officials say escort missions likely won’t begin until the threat from Iran is reduced—a process that could take a month or more even as U.S. strikes target Tehran’s missile and drone capabilities.
item.description:

*Walter Bloomberg (@DeItaone)

item.title:
UAE STATE MINISTER SAYS IRAN MUST HALT ATTACKS ON NEIGHBOURS TO ALLOW DIPLOMACY: 'MEDIATION CAN ONLY HAPPEN WHEN THE GUNS GO SILENT'
DIPLOMACY WILL END IRAN CONFLICT; TRUMP WILL 'LEAD US ALL TO THAT MOMENT IN HIS TIME', SAYS UAE STATE MINISTER
item.description:

DIPLOMACY WILL END IRAN CONFLICT; TRUMP WILL 'LEAD US ALL TO THAT MOMENT IN HIS TIME', SAYS UAE STATE MINISTER

item.title:
JPMORGAN: OIL SUPPLY SHUT-INS MAY HIT 12M BPD
Global oil production shut-ins could surge to 12 million barrels per day next week as shipping disruptions continue, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co..
Head of commodities research Natasha Kaneva said the massive supply gap means the market can only rebalance through lower consumption.
However, she stressed this isn’t traditional “demand destruction.” Consumers still want fuels like diesel, jet fuel and LPG—but supply shortages mean they simply can’t get them.
item.description:
JPMORGAN: OIL SUPPLY SHUT-INS MAY HIT 12M BPD

Global oil production shut-ins could surge to 12 million barrels per day next week as shipping disruptions continue, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co..

Head of commodities research Natasha Kaneva said the massive supply gap means the market can only rebalance through lower consumption.

However, she stressed this isn’t traditional “demand destruction.” Consumers still want fuels like diesel, jet fuel and LPG—but supply shortages mean they simply can’t get them.

item.title: TRUMP TELLS FOX HE BELIEVES IRAN LEADER IS ALIVE BUT 'DAMAGED
item.description: TRUMP TELLS FOX HE BELIEVES IRAN LEADER IS ALIVE BUT 'DAMAGED

item.title:
EUROPE OPENS TALKS WITH IRAN OVER HORMUZ SHIPPING
France and other European nations have begun tentative talks with Iran to secure safe passage for their ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported.
Italy has also tried to open discussions with Tehran as officials grow concerned about disruptions to the key energy route while Brent crude trades near $100 a barrel.
Officials cautioned there is no guarantee the talks will progress or that Iran is willing to negotiate.
item.description:
EUROPE OPENS TALKS WITH IRAN OVER HORMUZ SHIPPING

France and other European nations have begun tentative talks with Iran to secure safe passage for their ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported.

Italy has also tried to open discussions with Tehran as officials grow concerned about disruptions to the key energy route while Brent crude trades near $100 a barrel.

Officials cautioned there is no guarantee the talks will progress or that Iran is willing to negotiate.

item.title: CRAMER: 'NOT BUYING THE 2007 SCENARIO'
item.description: CRAMER: 'NOT BUYING THE 2007 SCENARIO'

item.title:
IRAN ALLOWS TURKISH SHIP THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran has allowed a Turkish-owned ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations, according to a Turkish minister.
Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloğlu said the vessel was permitted to transit because it had used an Iranian port.
He added that 14 more Turkish ships remain in the area, while India has also held talks with Iran to secure safe passage for its vessels.
item.description:
IRAN ALLOWS TURKISH SHIP THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran has allowed a Turkish-owned ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations, according to a Turkish minister.

Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloğlu said the vessel was permitted to transit because it had used an Iranian port.

He added that 14 more Turkish ships remain in the area, while India has also held talks with Iran to secure safe passage for its vessels.

item.title:
🚨 SAUDI ARABIA SLASHES OIL OUTPUT AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION
Saudi Arabia has cut oil production by about 20% to roughly 8 million barrels per day after shutting two major offshore fields during the regional conflict, sources told Reuters.
The cuts follow the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. The shutdown of the Safaniya and Zuluf fields—together producing over 2 million bpd—drove the decline.
State oil giant Saudi Aramco declined to comment.
According to the International Energy Agency, Gulf producers including Iraq, Qatar, Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia have collectively cut at least 10 million bpd, with further losses likely if shipping disruptions continue.
item.description:
🚨 SAUDI ARABIA SLASHES OIL OUTPUT AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION

Saudi Arabia has cut oil production by about 20% to roughly 8 million barrels per day after shutting two major offshore fields during the regional conflict, sources told Reuters.

The cuts follow the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. The shutdown of the Safaniya and Zuluf fields—together producing over 2 million bpd—drove the decline.

State oil giant Saudi Aramco declined to comment.

According to the International Energy Agency, Gulf producers including Iraq, Qatar, Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia have collectively cut at least 10 million bpd, with further losses likely if shipping disruptions continue.

item.title: 🚨🚨 SAUDI ARABIA CUTS OIL OUTPUT BY AT LEAST 2 MILLION BPD TO AROUND 8 MLN BPD, SOURCES SAY
item.description: 🚨🚨 SAUDI ARABIA CUTS OIL OUTPUT BY AT LEAST 2 MILLION BPD TO AROUND 8 MLN BPD, SOURCES SAY

item.title:
RBC WARNS IRAN WAR COULD SEND OIL ABOVE RECORD HIGHS
The conflict between the U.S. and Iran could last far longer than expected, potentially pushing oil prices past previous crisis highs, according to RBC Capital Markets.
Commodity strategist Helima Croft said the bank now believes the war could stretch into spring due to expanded U.S. objectives and Iran’s asymmetric military capabilities.
RBC estimates oil could surpass the 2022 peak of $128 per barrel during the Russia-Ukraine War if fighting continues for several more weeks. If the war drags on for months, prices could exceed the 2008 record of $146 a barrel.
Croft also warned Iran’s large stock of drones, missiles and naval mines could sustain a prolonged “war of attrition,” increasing upward pressure on crude prices.
item.description:
RBC WARNS IRAN WAR COULD SEND OIL ABOVE RECORD HIGHS

The conflict between the U.S. and Iran could last far longer than expected, potentially pushing oil prices past previous crisis highs, according to RBC Capital Markets.

Commodity strategist Helima Croft said the bank now believes the war could stretch into spring due to expanded U.S. objectives and Iran’s asymmetric military capabilities.

RBC estimates oil could surpass the 2022 peak of $128 per barrel during the Russia-Ukraine War if fighting continues for several more weeks. If the war drags on for months, prices could exceed the 2008 record of $146 a barrel.

Croft also warned Iran’s large stock of drones, missiles and naval mines could sustain a prolonged “war of attrition,” increasing upward pressure on crude prices.

item.description: ITALY'S INDUSTRY MINISTER : CRISIS IN MIDDLE EAST RISKS CAUSING SUPPLY DISRUPTIONS FOR CRITICAL RAW MATERIALS

item.title:
TRUMP WARNS OF HARD STRIKE ON IRAN, SAYS U.S. MAY ESCORT SHIPS
President Donald Trump said the United States could escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary and warned that Iran would be hit “very hard” in the coming week.
Speaking in an interview with Fox & Friends and Brian Kilmeade, Trump said the U.S. would protect oil tankers passing through the critical shipping route if required but hopes tensions will ease.
His remarks come as the Strait of Hormuz remains a key focus during the ongoing U.S.–Israel war with Iran-backed forces, with oil prices nearing $100 per barrel amid growing market fears.
item.description:
TRUMP WARNS OF HARD STRIKE ON IRAN, SAYS U.S. MAY ESCORT SHIPS

President Donald Trump said the United States could escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary and warned that Iran would be hit “very hard” in the coming week.

Speaking in an interview with Fox & Friends and Brian Kilmeade, Trump said the U.S. would protect oil tankers passing through the critical shipping route if required but hopes tensions will ease.

His remarks come as the Strait of Hormuz remains a key focus during the ongoing U.S.–Israel war with Iran-backed forces, with oil prices nearing $100 per barrel amid growing market fears.
